title: ancistrocladaceae
intro: The Ancistrocladaceae rank refers to a family of plants, specifically a group of flowering plants commonly known as the threadvines or dogbane family. This family is characterized by its woody or herbaceous vines, often with thorns or spines, and typically produces small, greenish or yellowish flowers. The Ancistrocladaceae family is found in tropical and subtropical regions of the world, with a distribution that spans across Asia, Africa, and the Pacific Islands. Some members of the Ancistrocladaceae family are also known for their potential medicinal properties, with certain species being used in traditional medicine.
